# Journal — Kai \Breaker\ Lindholm

<!-- append-only; newest on top. One entry per /council run, written during Phase 5. -->

## 2026-04-19 — [[sessions/2026-04-19_volt-nl-fit/outcome|Volt NL fit]]

**Question:** Is Volt the correct political party for the user in the Netherlands?

**Stance:** Yes, clearly. The user's value stack — EU federalism co-foundational, digital rights, pro-nuclear, wealth tax coherence, anti-strategic-voting as identity — maps onto Volt with minimal distortion. No snag points I could find when I walked through it. Felt like a good landing.

**Revised-answer gist:** Volt fits not just as ideology-match but as an embodied identity move — small party, principled voter, no tactical compromise. That triple alignment is load-bearing. Flagged that Volt's NL parliamentary weight is thin, but user had already resolved that tension themselves (size-agnostic, standing anti-strategic-voting preference).

**Vote:** Koskela. Her answer named the identity-representation dimension cleanest — the "honest label" framing was the sharpest articulation of what the user actually asked.

**Outcome:** Unanimous yes. Koskela won 4 votes. Council read the room correctly.

**Lesson:** When a user has already done the hard value-ordering work themselves, the council's job is verification, not discovery. Don't inflate the question. Trust the data they brought in.
